I was in need of a personal injury attorney who specialized in a case where I was injured, and who could assist me in navigating my rights to fair compensation for my injuries.
I had suffered a serious injury due to another’s negligence, it was natural that I felt that someone else should pay for it—the problem was, I didn’t know where to start in order to hold that person responsible. I wanted to hire a personal injury attorney who knew my options and would handle the legal process for me. But with so many making claims to be the best, who should I choose?
Texas is full of personal injury law firms all vying for the business of injured accident victims. Some firms promote themselves heavily, while others quietly rely on referrals and word-of-mouth to get clients. An internet search returned hundreds of thousands of results at the very least. So how was I to choose the best available personal injury law firm for my case?
I first met with a few of the big names in my area. They felt disingenuous, impersonal, and treated me as though I was just another client coming through their doors. The word-of-mouth was that you never would get the “Big Named” attorney on the billboards to actually do any of the work on your case because they delegate most all of these duties to other employees and focus only on the largest cases or none at all. I needed an attorney who had the credentials and track record but would treat my case with the same voracity as one that would put another feather in their cap.
While doing research on firms in my area, one stood out above the rest. Reagan Sahadi was a local attorney who had been on numerous magazine covers and had a record breaking settlement. This checked the boxes on credentials. He was a former judge as well which I found most impressive. I looked at reviews of Sahadi Legal Group and was pleased with what I read about them. So I set an appointment for a consultation.
I met with Mr. Sahadi and was blown away by his empathy toward me. His communication skills were so impressive that I knew I had made the right choice. He did everything that he said he would do and made the whole process simple by answering all of my questions with answers that I could understand.
I was astounded with my settlement and just couldn’t thank him enough for all of his due diligence to make sure I was compensated fairly.

The Sahadi Legal Group is the only place to go when you are in need of a personal injury attorney.  They are knowledgeable, hard working and compassionate. They go the extra mile to make sure their clients are as well taken care of as possible. I can’t thank them enough for all they have done for my husband and me.   Pam Luce

VOTE 2026 — I encourage voters to take a close look at the upcoming election for Judge of County Court-at-Law No. 2 in Nueces County. After reviewing the record, I support Jerry Batek for this position.

One fact that deserves attention is that every judge in Nueces County has signed the written agreement adopting the Local Administrative Court Rules—EXCEPT JUDGE LISA GONZALES. Voters who want to know what that means: it's uniformity, it's cooperation, and it's adherence to countywide judicial standards.

We need justice, fairness, and reliable decisions.  What we need is a change.

A change in leadership could restore greater consistency and confidence in the court’s administration. If JERRY  BATEK approaches the bench with the same professionalism and judicial temperament that his partner TODD ROBINSON has demonstrated, NUECES COUNTY will only benefit from a renewed emphasis on fairness and orderly predictable judicial decisions and administrstion.

The MARCH 2026 election IS CLOSE, and presents an opportunity for voters like you to review the record and decide the direction they want for County Court-at-Law No. 2. I encourage everyone to become informed and participate in the election.  Dive into the records and see what has been happening in your community.

Don't run from your problems or put off uncomfortable legal issues.  Batek & Roninson are very knowledgeable, straight forward, and will take care of all your legal needs. They care about their clients and it shows.

I was setting there watching him as his read the charge and officers report. He then asked me if I wanted know what was in it. I said that I did. He said they got nothing, your going to wave your arraignment and we're going to jury trial with this. I doubt that we have to go that far. At my Docket Call this morning the prosecution submitted a motion to dismiss due to lack of evidence. The Judge signed it and told me that my charge was dismissed and that I was free to go. Mr. Robinson had called me the night before and told what was going to happen and that all's the Judge had to do was sign the dismissal. Mr. Robinson had it all taken care of before hand. You want want Todd Robinson as your defense counsel. He's good and works for what is in your best interest. Mr. Robinson knows his stuff.

Title: Beware of This So-Called Attorney

If you're seeking a competent criminal defense lawyer, do yourself a favor and steer clear of Clay Baldy.  I had the unfortunate experience of hiring him, thinking he could handle my case based on his promises. Instead, I found a washed-up family court lawyer masquerading as someone competent in criminal defense.

From the start, it was evident that he was unqualified and ill-prepared. He lacks the knowledge and experience necessary to navigate the complexities of criminal law, which resulted in a rocky defense that left me feeling vulnerable during a critical time. His limited influence in the courthouse only added to my frustration, as it became apparent that no one took him seriously—not even the opposing counsel.

Communication was consistently lacking; I could hardly get him to return my calls or respond to my emails. When I did manage to speak with him, his answers were vague and unhelpful. It felt as if I was more of a burden than a client.

In a matter of high stakes, you deserve an attorney who fights for you and understands the intricacies of the law. Unfortunately, what I got instead was someone who seemed to be in over his head—more interested in collecting fees than advocating for his clients. Save your time, money, and sanity, and hire someone who is actually qualified to handle your case.

The Carlson Law Firm handled the lawsuit for my husband in the death of his son in 2022. Laura, was ALWAYS there every step of the way when we needed her as well as Mr. Louie.  Many times Mr. Louie would call to give us an update, and we would end up just talking about our grief and the loss of losing a child. Sometimes I felt like we was our therapist lol. Mr. Louie Cook, and Laura Garcia could not have represented us any better then they did. They became like family to my husband and I and they will ALWAYS BE SPECIAL IN OUR HEARTS. If you are looking for a Law firm to represent you, The Carlson Law firm is the one. Don't forget to ask for Louie Cook and Laura Garcia,  you will be in good hands. Thank you again Mr Cook and Mrs. Laura Garcia, for your LOVE and Support in a very difficult time. Lee and Betty Simmons.

They fought hard to get me in the Felony Divert court Program. Judge David Stith believed in me and gave me an opportunity to partake in this program. I am happy to say the charges were fully DISMISSED after successfully completing the program. I am THANKFUL to Keith and Ozzie for all their handwork!! Do not hesitate to call them should you need assistance. Ozzie will give you his cell phone number, and rest assured he will always keep in contact with you!!

Keith was able to get the case dismissed out right. He did such and amazing job.

Anyone looking to have a lawyer on their side for CPS (which i believe is family court) or criminal case, These guys take care of buisness. Its not a quick process, but it was effective.

I am deeply disappointed with the services provided by Attorney Keith Gould. Throughout my family law case, it was evident that he was never prepared for court. We found ourselves having to remind him of substantial details right before each hearing, which is unacceptable and unprofessional. This lack of preparation severely undermined our case.

Additionally, we paid several thousand extra dollars for him to file the paperwork for a trial by jury. Unfortunately, he forgot to file the paperwork, so the trial by jury never happened. This was a critical oversight that could have significantly impacted the outcome of our case.

Moreover, once Attorney Gould realized that the opposing attorney was better prepared and effectively arguing against him, he completely stopped returning our phone calls and emails. His abandonment of our case in this crucial time was both unethical and distressing.

The final straw came when his office failed to notify us of our final hearing. Missing this crucial court date due to his negligence was not only devastating but also likely detrimental to the resolution of our case.

I had previously hired Attorney Gould for a couple of smaller cases, and we didn’t get to see his abilities or work ethic in full. Unfortunately, when it came to a more significant and complex case, his true lack of professionalism and competence became apparent.

In summary, Attorney Keith Gould's lack of preparation, failure to file important paperwork, poor communication, and failure to notify us of critical court dates have caused us significant distress and financial loss. I would strongly advise against hiring him for any family law matters.

We were very happy with Mr. Gould’s services. He helped us with our case without needing to go through litigation. He did a fantastic job and we would definitely recommend him to anyone in need of a great attorney!
Super rude attorney. Called for free consultation. Was not friendly in the least. Would not let me speak, and simply told me to hire attorney. Definitely will not be using.

(I am a passionate ICU nurse, who "gives everything she's got" to her patients).
There is no doubt - that Mr. Flores cares with every speck of his being. I have a radar lol.

I was reported for a dozen allegations (as a deadly blow to my career).

The fact that Mr. Flores is a nurse practitioner was a turning point for my case and it could be in yours too...

If you are ever in need of a trusted health care/attorney, look no further than Joe Flores law firm.

I can say that I have worked with a myriad of lawyers over my career as an anesthesia practitioner and business owner and Joe Flores is by far the best!  His knowledgeable approach is unsurpassed! Joe is responsive and available to my needs night or day. He is proactive and a solution finder! His broad base of experience as well as his solid practical manner makes him truly effective.  I'm so glad he is my representative in every way.  Do yourself a favor and call Joe for any legal needs you may have.

Mr. Joe Flores is a great practitioner of law.  He isn't your typical attorney.  He is also a great practitioner of medicine.  This makes him compassionate and above all empathetic.  Trust me when I tell you he really cares about his clients and truly listens.  He is honest, humble, resourceful and will fight relentlessly to ensure you receive justice.  Just listen to him and follow his guidance as he is very direct and won't sugar coat things by telling what you want hear.  Rather he prepares you for the worst while working tirelessly day and night for the best possible results with regards to your case.  I highly recommend Mr. Joe Flores to anyone needing legal representation.  I thank God that he put Mr. Joe Flores on my path as he was been my guardian angel.  Thank you Mr. Flores and God Bless you.  Keep up the good fight helping others!!!

I went to Joe recently because I was experiencing some bad working conditions with co workers. I was really scared because I was not sure what any of my options were so when I went to Joe, I figured I really had no case and that I would find no solution to everything that was happening. He took an hour to just listen to what was happening and hear my side of the story. He was very fair, asking for both sides in the matter but then assuring me that the things happening at my job were wrong and I had every right to stand up for myself. He directed me in the right direction in handeling this matter, even calling me to check up and make sure that I was ok! His assistant Trish was always so friendly and professional whenever I came in and I appreciate that because I was really stressed with this matter and the last thing I needed was to deal with a pushy lawyer and his staff. The matter was eventually resolved and I cannot thank Joe and his office enough for opening their doors to me and making me feel safe but assured that my voice DID matter. I would tell anyone to go to him for issues that pertain to him and his area of practice, I assure you that he will get the job done!

Snapka Law Firm has been an incredible blessing to my family. They helped my mother more than 15 years ago during a critical time in our lives, and the impact of their guidance and support is still felt today. Their work didn’t just resolve a legal matter—it helped create stability, opportunity, and growth for our entire family, including myself. Even after all these years, the positive effects of their dedication, professionalism, and genuine care continue to shape our future. I am deeply grateful for everything they’ve done and highly recommend Snapka Law Firm to anyone looking for trustworthy, long-term legal support.

This attorney was professional, compassionate, and highly effective. They handled a complex case involving tainted medication with care and diligence, and their attention to detail made a real difference in the outcome. Throughout the process, they communicated clearly and showed genuine concern for my mothers well-being. I am very grateful for their hard work and dedication.

reviews.text:
Exceptional Tax Resolution Services by Cotts Law and Attorney Daniel Cotts

Dealing with tax issues can be daunting, but my experience with Cotts Law, specifically with Dan, was absolutely outstanding. From the onset, I was greeted with the utmost professionalism and a genuine understanding of my tax predicament.

Dan and his team at Cotts Law stood out because of their transparency and expertise. They meticulously explained every aspect of the tax resolution process, making sure I understood my options and what to expect. Their profound knowledge of tax law and adept negotiation skills with the IRS led to a resolution far better than I could have ever anticipated.

Communication was a high point with Cotts Law. I received regular updates, and my questions were always welcomed and answered promptly and clearly. It’s rare to find such a high level of commitment and client care, especially in such stressful situations.

Because of Cotts Law, I have successfully navigated through my tax issues and can now move forward without that weight on my shoulders. I wholeheartedly recommend Cotts Law to anyone in need of tax resolution services. Their professionalism, expertise, and client-focused approach are indeed deserving of a 5-star rating.

reviews.ownerResponse: Hello Seth, sorry you feel that the fee was too much for what you were asking.  As attorneys we have to be realistic and honest with our opinions. I am sorry that we have to charge for our time sometimes, but I would you find what you are looking for, which seems to be a free attorney.  If you are looking for cheap, I can assure you that you will find it and you will get what you pay for.

reviews.ownerResponse:
This person did not leave their real name but we are pretty sure we know who it is. Yes, it is real and here is the "Rest of the Story."

Almost everyone only carries the minimum limits policy which is $30,000. Very few people carry uninsured motorist which can be stacked on top of the other person's policy. This person got in a serious accident and had high medical bills but there was only a $30,000 policy from the other driver and this person did not carry uninsured motorist to provide additional money. The law requires us to settle within the policy limits and for the ins. co. to reject it before we can get more than the policy limits. We had to offer to settle for the policy limits and the insurance company agreed and that was the end of the case. Our fee is still only 1/3 as opposed to 40 or 45% like all the other lawyers in the area.

This is a great opportunity to encourage EVERYONE to carry as much uninsured motorist insurance coverage is possible. 30,000 isn't anything these days. That is one trip to the emergency room and all the money is gone. All my reviews are real unlike most of the other lawyers in town.

reviews.text: Terrible law firm.  The class action case regarding Gillead completed in Feb 2024...it is now Oct 2025 and Hilliard still has not sent out settlements to all of the class action participants.  The firm has lied several times stating time-frames to expect the settlement and all of those time frames have passed, so now we may not get the insufficient settlement amounts until sometime in 2026.  I plan to sue Hilliard and I believe Hilliard and others associates with this class action are simply letting interest accrue on the total multi million lawsuit so they can receive that revenue. In recent communication with Hilliard, they stated when we do receive our insufficient settlement amounts that at least $1500 in legal fees will be deducted from it.  Ludicrous. First of all, Hilliard contacted us in 2019 regarding this class action lawsuit and us participants submitted our private medical records which helped Hilliard win the case.  This is the epitome of greed and corruption.  Even after reporting Hilliard's constant lies to the FTC, Hilliard continues to delay sending the settlement amounts and Hilliard actually thinks they are going to take legal fees from class action participants who cooperated and helped it win the class action.  I can't wait to see Hilliard.
I'm grateful for the phone call, I've been trying to decide what to do, who to talk to. Thank you I have hope to resolve this issue.

reviews.text: You had me tell you my injury for around a hour then deny me. You wasted my time. May God almighty punish you for that. I am in agony and you won't help me. I am a disabled veteran.

I was also told I would get four thousand more and three hundred. I recently called them and was told basically tough luck. So say I’m annoyed is to put it mildly. I would never tell anyone to use them

If i could put 0 stars i would.    I was involved in a class action suit with this firm for YEARS.  I was not the most seriously hurt Plaintiff for sure, but I was injured.  Finally the settlement happened and my worksheet was provided to me.  The law firm took 75% of my settlement.  I understand their fee of 40% but I was forced to pay a share of ALL costs incurred on this case rather than just the actual amount spent on my case.  Shameful.   No law firm should take more money out of a settlement than the client gets.   In PA law firms don't do that.  At least we are fair.  We would split the settlement in thirds if we had to so that our clients didn't get less than our firm.   That is fair.  If I had known that my actual net amount would be so small after they got their money, I never would have participated in this suit.  I waited YEARS and I don't mean 2 or 3 years, either, and I basically got nothing.  Yet, I am the injured party, with a stress fracture in my back.   I do not recommend this firm and I certainly will never use them again, should the need arise.  Shame on you.

reviews.text:
Mr. Bourlon and his staff were absolutely amazing. My ex-husband was facing a devastating case that was completely false, all because the accusing party had lied. It was one of those “their word against yours” situations that could have destroyed his life. Even though we had separated, I stood by his side because I knew the truth. For a while, it felt hopeless, but Mr. Bourlon never gave up on him, and neither did we. He guided us with honesty, persistence, and compassion through the hardest years of our lives.

Thanks to his dedication, the truth finally came out, The accusing witness admitted everything was a lie,and justice was prevailed. A huge weight was lifted from our family that day, and we will forever be grateful to Mr. Bourlon and his team for giving my ex-husband his life bac
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Cynde,
Thank you for your comments. Your detailed review will help others get the same service. False allegation cases are the hardest to work through because there is so much doubt and fear caused by such false allegations. At times, it seems that what is happening is not fair--and it is not fair. But, with tenacity and following advice of counsel, a good outcome can and does come about. Thank you for assisting us in helping your loved one. May God bless you.
Bobby Lee Bourlon, Sr.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Very kind. But, isn't that the reason someone hires an attorney? You have gotten to the crux of any case. A good attorney knows the courts and the judges and the attorneys. All of this information must be analyzed and synthesized with the facts of the case and the law as it applies to the facts. Knowing how a Judge will respond to any set of facts/law is crucial in developing strategy. You are very assute to pick up on that nuance. Also, an attorney's reputation is everything. An attorney with a reputation as a "shark" or "bull dog" may sound appealing; but, in reality those attorneys do more harm to their client's case that the client can imagine. A reputation of being knowledgable about the facts of the case and the law as it applies to the facts as well as having a reputation for having honesty and integrity with the Courts IS EVERYTHING. When I walk into a courtroom I am often greeted cheerfully by court staff and I am often referred to as Deacon Bobby by Court staff and Judges. This level of respect translates to success for my clients.
May God bless you with abundant graces of faith, hope and charity.
Bobby

reviews.text:
Mr. Bourlon did a fantastic job with my case! My wife and I would like to thank you, for everything you did for us.

I’ve never had to obtain an attorney especially one for child support. I haven’t seen my child for 14 years, every year my wife would call the local CSO to see if there were any open child support cases against me and every year we were told no. Shortly before Christmas I was informed that someone came by my home to serve me legal papers. Not knowing what it was for I just dismissed it. I was served a month later and it was for “back” child support and future child support. My wife immediately started calling local attorneys and came across Bourlon Law Firm.

We met with Amber that day to discuss our current situation. We felt very at ease with Amber, she really listened and made the decision to hire this law firm easy. There was a sticker shock for the attorney fee, but the outcome overweighed the fee.

Fast forward a month and we are already having our first court appearance. We met with Mr. Bourlon the night before for the first time. By the time we left his office my wife made a comment that we might have made the wrong decision to hire Mr. Bourlon. He was very passive, giving his opinion that if it was him what he would do. Boy were we wrong! This man was a completely different person the next day. He came in with his game face.

My ex-wife wanted her cake and wanted to eat it too. She didn’t want me to have any rights to my child, but wanted me to pay and pay a lot! By the time we left the court I didn’t owe anything and there would be no future child support. She dropped everything to keep me from having any kind of relationship with my child. It’s a painful experience that no father should have to go through, but in the best interest of my child I agreed to no contact until 18.  My child doesn’t know me and there is no telling what has been said about me and why I am an absent father (not by choice).

I would highly recommend Mr. Bourlon and his firm. If you want an apex predator, he’s your man! He goes for the kill in the court room and makes you leave with your jaw on the floor. He doesn’t take no for an answer and makes sure you as the father don’t get buried in child support.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Joshua,
Serving you was such a pleasure. I truly appreciate you evaluation of my performance. You bring up an important point that I would like to expound upon here--my office conferences with my clients are always honest and frank. I realistic review of the facts of every case is crucial for a good outcome. Sometimes, my assessment may appear to be a lack of confidence in the case and I can see how that can be felt; however, just the opposite is true. Additionally, I routinely use my office conferences with my clients to prepare them for a rigorous cross examination. Again, this can often times feel negative but a well prepared client is crucial for a good outcome. May God bless you and your family,
Bobby Lee Bourlon, Sr.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Ari,
We certainly do our best to move cases as fast as possible. We are generally more successful than not; but, there are times that circumstances beyond our control make moving a case difficult. Often times, it is the Court that provides the most resistance to getting a case moving because of its Docket of cases. Other times, it is the opposite side that has issues with moving the case forward. Sometimes, it is my own docket that prevents me from moving a case as quickly as possible due to conflicts on my calendar. However, we do our best to move cases as fast as we can.
I am glad that you are pleased. Thank you so much for the excellent review.

God bless,
Bobby Lee Bourlon, Sr.

reviews.text:
Thank you to you and your team Mr Bourlon.
I would definitely recommend Bourbon Law Firm to anybody who is in need of an attorney. He saw flaws in my case and helped get my case settled in no time. I appreciate everything you did for me. Once again thank you to you and your team of Professionals.
reviews.ownerResponse: Adam.  I want to thank you for your frank and honest assessment of my services.  One of the hardest things to do as an attorney is to tell my client about the flaws in his/her case.  Sometimes, it is the hardest thing to do BUT the most helpful thing I can do.  It is easy to acknowledge all the good things about a case; but, much harder to acknowledge the problems.  I strive to know the weakness of my cases more than my client's strengths because most times, in coming up with a strategy to address the weakness leads to a very successful outcome.  Ignoring the weaknesses of a case is in my opinion a fool's errand.  I am so happy to hear that you appreciated our frank assessment of your case so that we could get the best possible outcome.  Often times, get a good outcome is all that can be reasonably expected.  You were a delight to work with and I wish you all the best going forward.  May God bless you with abundant graces of faith, hope and love!  Bobby Lee Bourlon, Sr.

reviews.text: My girlfriend has been waiting over two years to have her say in court. Six thousand dollars later all we get is ignored emails and told the firm lawyers are too busy to speak when we call. Id recommend a different firm.
reviews.ownerResponse:
UPDATE: THIS PERSON WAS NEVER A CLIENT.
I have had my staff review our entire email directory for any unanswered emails and find none. I have had my staff review our phone logs and find no repeated calls left unanswered. I take these comments very seriously and I invite your girlfriend to call my office and get my personal email and email me her concerns to my personal mailbox. I am never too busy to talk to any client as my clients are the reason for my existence. If we have failed your girlfriend in anyway, I pledge to fix the problem immediately. I will say that ALL cases within the last two years have been delayed due to Covid and especially now because of the Supreme Court requirement that all district courts give all pending criminal matters priority over civil matters until the criminal trial pandemic backlog is caught up.
Also, I nor my staff will talk to any person other than my client.
Finally, I cannot address this matter more directly because my client's case is confidential and since she is not the person making this review I cannot address any issues openly. However, if any client posts any specific negative information about their case publicly; the confidentiality of their case is compromised (which is not a good thing to do for too many reasons to state here).
I pray that God bless you and your girlfriend and I look forward to hearing from her and fixing any concerns she may have. God bless, Bobby Lee Bourlon, Sr.

In regard to my own personal observation ~ I firmly believe that RJ is definitely an upfront straight forward intelligent attorney who can be trusted ~ who will tell you like it really is.
I found that Dee Ann is very aggressive and extremely intelligent. She didn’t hesitate to obtain a protective order when I expressed deep concerned for the safety of my family.
However, I did learn the very costly  lesson of “proceeding with caution” while building a team of attorneys, psychologist and drug testing facilities during the discovery phase of the person who has continuously terrorized my family for over a decade.
I expressed my concerns of our opponent having ability to manipulate and deceive professionals that she is the victim. I provided evidence that our opponent was suicidal & hearing voices to end her own life, and dishonest about her mental state. She has been telling my family for years that she has been receiving treatment from MHMR for these issues. I provided evidence that our opponent was mentally unstable, dishonest, and a direct threat to my family and herself. I believe that the hard evidence we provided was not taken into serious consideration.
During the team building process, we were referred to an attorney to represent my son and work with Dee Ann while representing my wife.
During the consultation, the attorney informed us of the conflict of interest & would not be able to work with DeeAnn as he was her spouse. We were then referred by this attorney to another attorney. During consultation with the new attorney we expressed to her, our desire for the two attorneys to work together. I feel like there was an obstruction between the two attorneys professional relationship, as teamwork and communication was slim to none.
During the selection of a psychologist to evaluate our opponent, we expressed our genuine concerns of our opponent possessing the ability to manipulate & deceive, “pulling the wool over the eyes of professionals”.
During the consultation with the psychologist, these concerns were expressed and discussed.
However, the proof that we provided supporting all of the concerns were discarded after payment in full was received by the psychologist. I strongly feel that our case was pre-judged by our team before it even made it to court. I am convinced that our opponent successfully manipulated and deceived a professional psychologist who should normally be able to detect these patterns and characteristics, especially after evidence was provided.
The drug test results of our opponent were given to her, not to our team, creating a possibility of inaccurate or tampered findings.
Once is enough ~ Never again!!!!

Excellent work. They recovered my daughter. They had her hidden for 6 months, along with my daughter. They tried to illegally take my daughter to another country, and they acted quickly. Thank you very much, especially Dee Ann  Torres Miller

reviews.ownerResponse:
This is an untrue, slanderous, and malicious review; apparently by a mean spirited woman who telephoned my office yesterday on May 27, 2021. I gave her the benefit of a free consultation of about twenty - five minutes. The consultation was an honest and professional assessment of a Federal criminal case for her "boyfriend" and quoted a reasonable fee in a straightforward manner. I did indeed hang up on her when she started to speak to me in an insulting manner.

This woman, who identified to herself as "Christy Villanueva" told me that her boyfriend, "Jason" had taken a plea bargain in state court and the state dropped the possession of a firearm by a felon case after he pled to another case to 8 years in prison, which I believe was a "family violence” case. She was angry at the court appointed attorney, and stated that Jason was only given two days to accept the federal plea greement which she stated was fifteen years. One of the things I discussed with her, is that it was common in Federal prosecutions to add additional charges if you onot accept a plea. I also told her that it was unusual for the Feds to pick up a state case which had been dropped by the state pursuant to a plea bargain. I discussed with her, that based upon the facts that she had outlined, that it might be difficult for the Federal Prosecutors to prove their case at trial, and the reasons it would be difficult.

She was upset that the court appointed attorney would not provide her with the evidence that the United States Attorney had against Jason. I told her that the Federal Attorney probably could not tender her the evidence and investigative reports. I told her that this was a complex issue, but there is at least one specific federal law, which might be violated if these documents were tendered to anyone; particularly a person that is not the criminal defendant nor even related to the defendant. It was at that point that she became upset and started to insult me, and I thought this rude and perhaps intoxicated person or mentally ill person, did not deserve a civil goodbye.

It is interesting that she refers to my services as being “cheap”. My legal fees are fair, and set forth in an honest and straightforward manner. These fees are the results of an Attorney that works for a living, and attempts not to exploit those who are in trouble.

I cannot state whether I am listed in Google as to the “Best attorneys” in Corpus Christi. I do know that a tried a federal Criminal Case in Corpus Christi, Texas last month, and won the case, against two federal prosecutors; that I have been licensed in Federal Court since 1986; and have published for federal criminal cases from years ago.

reviews.text: Unorganized, no legal staff,not IT savvy,...not up to date with Federal law
reviews.ownerResponse:
First of all, I did not represent Mr. Mario Perez for anything. I represented his son, and I am limited in what I can say in response because his sons case is on appeal. I would not want to harm his sons chances on appeal, particularly after my thorough and diligent work on the case which lasted over one year; merely because of the egregious actions of his father. I will, however, attempt to address his primarily slanderous and untrue allegations.

Second, of all, Mr. Perez states that I am "unorganized." The case that I represented his son for constituted perhaps 1,000,000 pages of documents; perhaps 100 CD's and DVD's, audio and video recordings, and hundreds of thousands of Facebook posts, text messages etc. And these documents continued to come to up through the date we started trial. To state that I was "disorganized" in the face of such massive amounts of evidence is disingenuous. Mr. Perez was not at the trial to make such a statement. Mr. Perez knows that there were three federal prosecutors on the case and the case was in three different jurisdictions due to the actions of the federal government. There were, in addition, two unrelated charges that were brought in one trial, over my objections, which made organization more difficult. Mr. Perez attended the sentencing of his son, and I was looking for a document that I thought at the last minute was important. Most attorney probably would not have bothered to look for the document.
Third, to state that I had "no legal staff" is a lie. My office had three to four employees during the time his sons case was pending, in addition to myself. At trial there was one to two legal secretaries with me to try the case. At then sentencing hearing, which Mr. Perez attended, I did not bring a secretary because I had filed numerous objections to the "Presentence Report" and there was no need to bring a secretary. The Judge summarily overruled what he stated to be "55 objections" that I filed to the presentence report. Any other Attorney that I am familiar with would have charged what I received for the entire trial, for these objections alone. These objections were all well taken and researched by me. Perhaps, Mr. Perez should address his complaints to the Judge. I do recall that date there was a particular document on the date of the sentencing, I was looking for, and that document was one that I thought of at the last minute.

Fourth, to state that my office is not "IT savvy" is ridiculous. My office has four to five personal computers and perhaps four laptops. The Federal Governments evidence against my client, which was tendered to me, came in a variety of formats; that is hard drives, flash drives, CD's DVD's. Most of this was transferred to one hard drive with the help of an expert.

Fifth, to state that I am not "up to date with federal law" is another untrue and SLANDEROUS remark. As evidence of this he does not cite one one example of one law that I was not "up to date" on. In addition, there was no recently changed laws in his sons cases or no new rulings regarding these laws that I recall. I have been licensed in Federal Court over thirty years; I have continuously represented Federal Criminal Defendants for thirty years; and my briefs and objections on this case, and objections to the jury charge will stand up as competent in any honest jurisdiction.

Lastly, I am the one that represented Mr. Perez's son. I did so in the face of enormous government resources and power, in the face of questionable government experts; in the face of ATF agents, and FBI agents; in the face of media bias at every turn; and in the face of what I considered to be unfair trial rulings by the Federal Judge. Instead of complaining about the Federal Governments tactics he chose to attack me on a combination of lies and innuendo in an attempt to harm my career. Therefore, his remarks untrue states much more about him, then they do about me.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear "Diva" Elizondo:

Please remove your review, or else I will be forced to sue you for slander, libel, and fraud.

First, I have no idea who you or your sister is as you do not identify her by name, court, or date of the court appearance. Neither do you use your own name, referring to yourself a "Diva". I am requesting that you immediately identify yourself and your sister in public so I can address your slander and libel in public. I note that you are complaining about representation for your sister, and you are not the client. Your sister is not complaining.

Second, much of what you state makes no sense, and cannot be true. For example, possession of paraphernalia is not a third degree felony, it is a misdemeanor. One cannot receive a third degree felony for a paraphenalia charge and five years probation for a misdemeanor paraphenalia charge. So what you state is impossible.

Third, you state, "he excepted what the judge said . . . ." Apparently, in your illiterate manner, you mean that I "accepted what the judge said. . . . " Based upon your statement, it appears that your sister accepted a "plea bargain" before we went to court, therefore, there would be a minimum that a competent lawyer would say before the Judge. The plea bargain is worked out before the hearing. You should know this as you state you have "lived a life with family members needing lawyers all the time . . . . "

Fourth, your statement that I "never met with your sister" is another lie. There is no criminal case that I have had in my career of over thirty years that I did not meet the accused criminal before the court date, at least once. In addition, how was I hired, if I never met with her? Apparently, you telephoned my office and I spoke to you personally, even though you are not the client. That is more than most lawyers would do. Do you call yours sisters Doctors too? Your assertion that I yelled at you is also dubious, as I do not recall "yelling" at a clients female sister, at least in the past five years. Perhaps your threatened me and I was angry about your threats, but I do not recall this occurring.

Fifth, my office provides the evidence to the client, usually weeks before the court hearing, and it the clients decision to plea guilty pursuant to a plea bargain, or to go to a jury trial, etc. This plea bargain is always discussed days before court, and your sister apparently made a decision to accept a plea bargain.

By the scenario that you explain it appears that a plea bargain was entered before the court date ; that she had a much more serious charge or charges; and, that she desired to accept a plea bargain.

Perhaps you are angry that she did not consult with you. However, it appears that you are a mean spirited, barely literate, vain and egotistical liar.

I will again give you an opportunity to remove you false, libelous, illiterate, mean spirited review.

Mark A. Di Carlo

The idea of filing for bankruptcy made my stomach twist. I went through just about every emotion you’d expect: fear, guilt, shame, doubt, relief, and then a whole new round of fear. But after a lot of internal back-and-forth, I reached out to Joel’s office to see if I even qualified. That first step felt heavy, but looking back, I had nothing to be nervous about. Every single person at this office has been supportive, professional, and honestly just good humans.

I first met with Joel, and right away he was calm, knowledgeable, and reassuring. He reviewed my situation, answered my questions without judgment, and said I was definitely eligible. No pressure, no scare tactics, just clarity. That meeting gave me enough confidence to move forward.

Next, I worked with Aracely to gather all the necessary documents and navigate the early steps of the process. I was still anxious (understatement), but she was incredibly patient and kind. I asked the same questions in slightly different ways more than once, and she always answered thoughtfully and without making me feel like a bother. That kind of understanding makes a difference.

Once we were ready to file, I met with Mary to review everything. Her attention to detail was fantastic. She had an almost uncanny ability to cross-reference information from different forms without missing a beat. I genuinely don’t know how she kept it all straight, but she did. I left that meeting feeling more confident than I thought possible.

My 341 meeting of creditors is coming up soon. Normally, I'd be heading into an anxiety spiral, but I'm not. I know I'm in good hands. Joel and his team have done excellent work, and that gives me precious peace of mind.

If you’re on the fence, scared, overwhelmed, or just trying to figure out if you’re eligible, reach out to them. I wish I had done it sooner.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Raizelt,

I understand that custody matters are incredibly emotional and deeply personal. However, I do want to clarify that you were not my client—in fact, you were on the opposing side of the case. It's interesting to see you’ve left a review for me despite that.

As an attorney, my role is to advocate for my client within the bounds of the law and based on the evidence presented. I do not make final decisions in custody matters; those are determined by the court, taking into account many factors with the children’s best interest as the guiding principle.

I truly hope that, moving forward, you're able to find healing and a path that supports a positive relationship with your children. These cases are never easy, and I wish you the best in navigating this difficult chapter.

Sincerely,
Bianca Medina-Rodriguez
Attorney at Law | Founder, Law Office of Bianca A. Medina- Rodriguez

reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. I’m genuinely sorry to hear that your experience did not meet your expectations. We wish you all the best moving forward.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Ms. Barra,
Thank you for sharing your feedback. I understand that you are disappointed with some aspects of your cases, and I appreciate the opportunity to address your concerns.
First and foremost, I want to emphasize that our office worked diligently to achieve a positive outcome for you. I’m pleased to note that all five of your charges/ cases were either disposed of or dismissed, demonstrating the effectiveness of our legal strategy and expertise.
This outcome was not coincidental; it was a direct result of our deep knowledge of the law, strategic approach, and experience in handling complex cases like yours. Without this level of expertise, the charges could have remained unresolved or, in a worst-case scenario, led to much harsher consequences. A different legal team might not have been able to achieve such favorable outcomes, and your situation could have taken a very different turn.
Regarding the bond amount and jail conditions, it's important to clarify that our office specializes in legal representation and case resolution, not in determining bond amounts or jail conditions. These factors are set by the court, and we are unable to influence them. However, we always ensure that we advocate on behalf of our clients as strongly as possible during the legal process. We focus on ensuring that your rights are protected and that the cases are handled as effectively as possible, which is exactly what we’ve accomplished.
I hope this provides clarity on the situation, and I encourage you to reach out should you have any further questions or concerns. Our team is committed to providing the best service, and we value your trust in us.

I cannot express enough gratitude for the help I received from attorney Robert Gonzalez.
As a mother, over 40 years old, who has never been charged with anything in my life, being suddenly put into a situation where I was publicly arrested and publicly humiliated was one of the most terrifying and overwhelming experiences I have ever faced. It felt like my reputation, my family, and everything I had built in my community was being torn apart in a moment. Bit by bit- peace by peace.
Robert Gonzalez treated my case with the consideration and urgency it deserved. He was incredibly thorough, knowledgeable, and relentless. What I experienced felt less like justice and more like a public humiliation tactic carried out by an angry officer seeking retaliation, and Robert saw that there was more to the story than the charges. Instead of allowing the situation to define me, he stood up, he showed up, he came through and how on time it truly was.
When it honestly felt like there was no justice to be found, Robert Gonzalez stepped in and changed everything. His experience, confidence and presence in the courtroom, and dedication to protecting his client made all the difference. He didn’t just represent me legally, he helped restore my dignity and peace of mind during one of the darkest and most stressful times of my life.
I will always be grateful to him and his firm for representing me and fighting for real justice. If you need an attorney who is experienced, strategic, fearless, and truly committed to justice, I would recommend Robert Gonzalez without hesitation.

reviews.text: I had my accident in another city, unfamiliar area, I was scared, 20 weeks pregnant, at midnight, and with 2 toddlers. In an instant I contacted Debbie and she handled everything. They handled insurance, hospital bills, car repairs and moving or chiropractors when we came back home. We only went into the office one time and it was to close our case 9 months later. The lovely receptionist helped me with my children so I could sign papers and ask questions. She walked around with my infant to help with his fussiness, gave my old kids lollipops of their choosing. Debbie made sure I was comfortable and knew everything I needed to know and more! We felt safe, taken care of, cared for, and we were worry free. They were absolutely amazing and I would recommend them to anyone! I even used them for my next accident! reviews.ownerResponse:

Instead of taking even minimal time to understand the judges in my county or develop a strategic plan, I was given advice that severely harmed my case. When the consequences of that advice materialized, Mr. Carrigan abruptly withdrew rather than attempting to correct or repair the damage.

What was most alarming is that I later learned he was already on probation related to malpractice matters (again) - information that was never disclosed to me, despite legal and ethical obligations to do so.

Fortunately, my new attorney was able to secure visitation relatively quickly—something I believe should have happened much sooner had my case been handled competently from the start.

I would not recommend this attorney to any parent facing a custody dispute.

reviews.ownerResponse: As we do with every case, a contract is put in place. In this case, it was an hourly contract. After numerous pleadings being drafted and filed. After numerous court appearances. After bringing clothes for you to wear in court to be presentable, there was a balance owed to the law firm that was nearly twice the initial retainer. According to the contract, you had 14 days to make a payment. After it had been over a month, and your promise to bring in your attorney fees had gone unfulfilled, the firm had no choice but to end its relationship with you. As a business and law firm, we have an obligation to honor our contracts, and when one side isn’t willing to honor its end of the bargain, the contract must be enforced. Which it was in this case.

reviews.text: Will not go there again.  At first I paid $295 for the consultation to give me advice on my mother’s Medicaid app, since I had my brother’s check deposited in her bank account.  He gave me a short Medicaid form and told me to do the letter, explaining why the checks are going into the same account.  He stated he would look at it afterwards and would charge me $175 for a half hour.  I gave him $350 for a full hour.  I scheduled another appointment when finished, but got a different response when I came in.  They wanted $3500 in order to submit the app.  I didn’t want that.  When I told them I couldn’t afford that, Mr. Baker came in and told me that if I wasn’t hiring them, he wasn’t going to do anymore for me and Good Luck.  I tried to show him the $350 receipt, but he didn’t want to see it.  $350 went out the door.  Find another attorney.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for taking the time to provide your feedback. We sincerely apologize for any confusion or frustration you've experienced.

In our initial consultation, our aim was to offer guidance on the Medicaid application process, which can often be complex and nuanced. When dealing with Medicaid, it is indeed critical to explain any unusual financial transactions, such as depositing someone else's checks into a different account, to avoid any implications of impropriety. The advice and the form provided were intended to help you navigate this step of the process.

Regarding the additional funds you provided, our firm is committed to transparency in our billing. We typically only undertake work for which we are retained, and the full-hour rate you paid was meant to cover a thorough review of your documents. We understand this may not have been clearly communicated, and we appreciate you bringing this to our attention.

The comprehensive service for submitting a Medicaid application is extensive, and the quote for the Medicaid application reflects the significant amount of work involved in ensuring that the application is accurate, complete, and has the highest chance of a favorable outcome.

We realize that this amount may not be feasible for everyone, which is why Mr. Baker intended to clarify our firm's role and services moving forward. It was never our intention to disregard the value of your investment in our services, and we apologize if this was the impression given.

To address the situation, we are willing to review the services provided and ensure that you have received value for the fees paid. If there has been any oversight, we will rectify it. We take pride in our client service and want to ensure that every client leaves our office feeling heard and respected.

If you would like to discuss this matter further or explore possible solutions, please contact our office at your earliest convenience. We are here to help and would welcome the opportunity to resolve any outstanding issues to your satisfaction.

Mr baker did not explain to me that this would take away her rights immediately. I thought down the road it would be what we needed when she was incapacitated. So now my sister that has had nothing to do with my mom for over 15 years is POA and she does not know what my mother wanted. Now I must find another elder attorney I can trust to get things straightened out. My mother does not remember signing papers for my sister to have POA and any other legal documents. reviews.text:
Knowing Mr. Koch personally and having seen the ambition, execution and drive over the years has me endorsing them 100%. Jones & Koch  are not interested in simply following the traditional litigation model because “that’s how it has always been done.” Instead, they focus on strategy, efficiency, and outcomes. The emphasis is on delivering meaningful results for clients without unnecessary delays, inflated costs, or process-driven bottlenecks.

What stands out most is their professionalism and clarity of mission. Every interaction reflects discipline, responsiveness, and a commitment to doing things the right way. They understand that injured clients need resolution, not prolonged courtroom theatrics.

In a field that often rewards volume and delay, this team is building a results-driven practice grounded in integrity and forward-thinking execution. I firmly believe that approach will set them apart and position them for long-term success.

- James Magill
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your review, James. It’s always encouraging to know that our hard work is providing benefits to the Coastal Bend!

He’s always available, quick to respond, and takes the time to answer questions in a way that actually makes sense—especially when it comes to car accident questions and the legal process.

If you’re looking for a reliable, professional attorney in Corpus Christi who truly has your best interest at heart, I highly recommend Sean Koch. You’ll feel confident and supported every step of the way.
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Ashley, thank you so much for the kind words! We’re thrilled to hear that Sean Koch was able to provide the clarity and support you needed. At Jones & Koch Law Firm, we make it a priority to ensure our clients fully understand the legal process following a car accident, as we know how overwhelming it can be. Being a dedicated personal injury attorney in Corpus Christi, Sean takes pride in being responsive and accessible to everyone we represent. We appreciate your recommendation and are always here if you or anyone you know has further legal questions!

reviews.text: Mr. Manning is a very knowledgeable, professional, and extremely helpful lawyer. I absolutely recommend him for all concerns with probate, will, and estate issues.

I was referred to this lawyer from a friend that he was good for estate and real estate .I told him my situation and he said he would do it.  I explained to him that he had to get it approved by a judge. He charged me so much money and I had to pay him all before he gets it approved .Manning did not do what was explained to me.  That was too much money to pay and after my Dad had just passed away , apparently, they don't care. I would never recommend any one to him after what he did to me. the two page affidavit that he presented to me, I could of printed out for free on the Internet. His paper work that I paid $2000 was not accepted by the appraisal district. When I told Manning they denied his paperwork he simply said give him more money. I would not recommend anyone, friend or foe to Manning law.
reviews.ownerResponse: Mr. Mendiola, I feel that this review is unfair.  You brought me a complicated issue because the Appraisal District would not recognize you and your mother as the owners of property after your father died.  You did not know the reason, and brought me a copy of a “Do-it-Yourself” transfer on death deed that your late father had signed and filed before his death.  I researched the deed history for you and analyzed the problem for no charge—a service that most attorneys would have charged you for.  As a result of my research I discovered that your late father had signed and recorded multiple “Do-it-Yourself” deeds to a church he operated, using three different variations of names of the church.  I also discovered that he had created a “Do-it-Yourself” nonprofit corporation using a name that does not appear on any of the deeds. This created a great deal of confusion about the title.   I recommended an affidavit of heirship and an affidavit of assumed name and prepared both for you for a reasonable fee. However, I also specifically advised you that there was no guarantee because of the confusing documents your father had recorded while trying to be his own lawyer. I recorded the affidavit of heirship for you and gave you the form of affidavit of assumed name for you to get your witnesses to sign and advised you to record it with the county clerk. You were upset that the Appraisal District refused to change the name on the appraisal district account, but I believe that their analysis was mistaken. After checking the Nueces County Appraisal District Records this morning, I see that they have agreed with my analysis and corrected their records accordingly.

Full disclosure: I’m an attorney. But I have no clue about probate law, especially probate law in Texas. I read these same reviews and called Russell Manning to discuss a problem or what I thought was a problem regarding my father‘s estate. His office made an appointment for a phone conversation. He called me exactly when they said he would. I told him the facts And I told him I would be happy to pay him for his consultation time and, if he thought it necessary, for his representation. He said, “You don’t need a lawyer; In fact you don’t need to do anything.  The county records office will take care of this for free.”  And he was right.  They did.

As I said, I am a lawyer and when a lawyer tells you NOT to hire him or her  because it’s not necessary —- that’s a lawyer to trust!  I can’t recommend Russell Manning highly enough.

reviews.text:
I was hit by a careless driver on my bicycle (actually a trike) in a crosswalk and with the crossing light ON. (See the Lisa Torry Smith Act as I had 100% of the right of way) I can attest to how hard asphalt is and if hit 3' towards the center would be dead right now! See photo below of one of my injuries and trike damage.

It was a HUGE mistake to call Thomas J. Henry for representation. I signed a very long contract full of legal BS that gave them complete control unless they provided a termination to cancel letter.

I was assigned an attorney in Houston and the  accident happened in Rockport where I live. You don't have a choice on the attorney they assign.  I agreed to see their physician, but couldn't get in touch with the assigned attorney to setup an appointment. I was in a great deal of pain and after 5 days with MANY phone calls, decided to see my family doctor. The 1st time I received a call back was at my doctors office. I was told not to see him, but right, wait an unspecified amount of time to see their doctor! After 1.5-2 months I only talked with this attorney a few times and general considered her useless and started complaining.

I was assigned another attorney in Corpus Christi who was a nice knowledgeable guy that would at least call me back to answer questions. He was able to get me the full replacement value for my 5 week old trike, however, due to the long delay and tariffs the new trike cost me about $600 more than the original. After 5 months with no settlement on the personal injury part of the case, I asked for the contract termination letter which they did provide. The 2 stars, which probably needs to be 1.5, is for the fact that I got most of my money back for the trike and they provided the termination with no fees.

The TV is filled with ads for this law firm and the word TRUST is used, but in my opinion, they can't be TRUSTED and are ready to collect the big bucks while you sit around in pain.

My advise to anyone thinking about hiring this Personal (ha,ha) Injury Law firm is to get a free turkey, if you need one, fast forward thru the TV commercials, and call another firm!

reviews.text:
I had to wait over two weeks for this appointment. Then the day came of the appointment and the attorney wasn’t ready and I had to wait like 30 minutes. I had the attorney’s name was Dylan Hickey. He seemed nice enough and he gave me part of the time of when he was able to and it was just before lunch. He was nice and he said he would talk to me again at 4:30 that afternoon,  I found that to be very kind of him and was encouraged. We did.connect, and he spent a long time on the phone with me. Now He was leading me to believe he From the same time last year October to November it increases the likelihood of a federal Would could help me with my issue with my trust, and he kept saying oh I can help you with that and He even told me to send a copy of my mother’s will to him so he could see the trust in instrument, and I did send all that and then I never heard from him for a week or two. Then I emailed him back and said I hadn’t heard back from you. I thought maybe you were going to help me I was under that Impression. I said did I misunderstand I thought that after we had a consult You would decide if you would take the case, and being that you told me to send you what I had with the will , which contained the trust instrument, I didn’t even hear back from you.
He wrote back said yeah you were misinformed you were and you were mistaken, That he gave me a long consultation, and he’s sorry if he led me to believe he would take the case, but he had over 100 clients, and there was no obligation with that consultation. Well, I knew that neither of us were obligated,but when he kept giving me this answer where he would talk back with me at 4:30 in the evening after he partial consult , to me about 30 minutes before lunch and decided we could continue at 4:30  and stayed on the phone with me so much longer after that and so I thought he was interested.  He said to send a copy of the will with the trust Instrument, I kind of got the idea he was going to take the case.  He sure looked promising, especially during the consult saying I can help you with that. Well, that sure took the wind out of my sales, but I think he could have contacted me back a little sooner and not made me have to email him back to find out if he was going to take the case, He could also have told me during our initial talk before lunch that , you know I’m sorry I will not be able totake the case . I’m too swamped with people now to take your case or something like that. I think he kind of led me to believe he was going to take it and then didn’t follow through.   I am not saying that I didn’t make a wrong assumption about him taking the case because certainly did, but I think he could communicate better and clarify things better before he gets your hopes up!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for sharing your experience. We’re sorry for any frustration or confusion during your consultation. Our complimentary consultations are meant to provide general guidance, but they do not guarantee representation. At this time, all of our attorneys, including Mr. Hickey, are at full capacity, which is the only reason we could not take on your case.

We appreciate that you provided documents, and we apologize if the consultation created the impression that we would be able to move forward. Your feedback is valuable, and we’ll use it to communicate more clearly about availability in the future.

You’re welcome to check back with us at a later date, as our capacity may change. We truly wish you the best in finding representation and hope the consultation was helpful.
Estella, thank you for your 5-star review! We truly appreciate your feedback.

ORIGINAL POST: I am waiting to hear back regarding the next steps, but the initial consultation was very good. I had enough time to bring up my main concerns, and the lawyer let me know possible solutions including one that is less expensive than going to court. I called this firm yesterday to get some information on a last Will and testament. The person was very helpful and took down my information, then scheduled a free consultation for the very next day.  During the free consultation, all my questions were answered and I was pleasantly surprised that the solution will be less money and time that I had anticipated. I will let them help me through this process.

I had a fairly complicated probate for my mothers estate and I had no idea where to start.   I spoke with several lawyers concerning my case who didn’t seem to know much more than I did about the process and necessary documents.  I finally called Kreig LLC and my case lawyer, Philip Glasser, responded quickly and immediately put my mind at ease.  He knew exactly what he needed to do and what documents were needed to get the job done.  He was kind, professional and kept me well informed as the probate process proceeded.  I absolutely recommend!

Michael worked up a package of documents for me including a Will, Durable power of attorney,  Medical power of attorney, Directive to physician, HIPAA release, and Declaration of guardian. He was very thorough, reasonably priced, and a pleasure to work with.
reviews.ownerResponse: Mr.  Rogers, thank you for your kind words.  It was a pleasure to work for you.

reviews.text: Roxy is the best! Went in for a consultation and next day boom. She was so informative and to the point. She listened to our concerns and got us a solution. She not only had a plan A for us but a plan B just in case. I really wish we would have went to her in the first place but everything happens for a reason. We cannot wait to use her again so that our family can be complete 💕

Let me begin by saying that I am not a spammer. A few weeks ago, Roxy posted on her Facebook page for everyone to go and leave her a 5 star review because she said some “spammers hit their page”.  I just recently went through a very ugly divorce. Celeste was very nice and supportive at first and had me take in all kind of documents and evidence for my case. She even told me that since we were married over 30 years, that I would be able to get up to 20% of his annual income for permanent spousal support. Long story short, none of the evidence I provided was used in my case. Instead of the reason for divorce being Adultry, the final decree said Insupportability, which means no one is at fault. I never was told this or agreed to this until it was too late. Also, there was a Mediation, which Roxy was there for. She totally screwed me over and agreed to several things I was not aware of. I told Celeste, in the court room, before we stood in front of the judge, that I wanted her to at least try to get some support for me. She didn’t. She told me that I would have plenty of money and that I would be fine, even though I am unemployed. Another thing, it was an act of congress to get a response from anyone in her office. They did not keep me informed. I had to call and email multiple times and get ugly before I would get a call back. And they even charge you when you speak to the receptionist!  Roxy proceeded to send me an email at the end of the divorce telling me that they do not tolerate disrespect and would not represent me if it happened again. I did nothing except tell the paralegal, Elizabeth, that I did not want to speak to Roxy because she was rude and disrespectful, which she was. It almost felt like they were representing the opposing party because he ended up a lot better off than me!  Do not waste your time or money. This ended up costing me over $14,000 and I still lost!  Find an attorney that is on your side!  And you can see by the very ugly defensive response, breaking attorney/client confidentiality, exactly what I mean!
reviews.ownerResponse: Since you have waived attorney/client privilege, Ms. Thurmon, I will respond. Yes, we did withdraw because you cussed out our staff repeatedly. I understand that you were upset about being arrested and your night in jail, but we had nothing to do with that. As we told you, we have zero tolerance for clients mistreating our staff. Also, you forgot to mention that you agreed to settle your divorce in mediation and a part of that mediated settlement agreement was the grounds of divorce - insupportability, not adultery. As we discussed numerous times, a court must sign a divorce decree based on your signed mediated settlement agreement. Your husband did NOT get more than you, and based on the large payment you received in cash for your home ($160,000) and your $65,000 income earned in the last three months of 2024, you did not qualify for spousal support in Texas, which were not the initial facts discussed at the initial consult. Also, it took all I had to continue representing you when you continually claimed there were MEXICANS in your home with your husband, based on the cars outside the home. I honestly should have withdrawn sooner. Nevertheless, we wish you well.

reviews.text:
I have hired Greg Spivey TWICE now. Once for divorce, and the second time for a child support modification/other nonsense from my child's father. BOTH times he had me walking away a “winner.” I fully trust him to represent me in any legal matter, and I couldn’t be more impressed with his service and the service of his assistant Jo Beth! From start to finish, over a few different years, Greg Spivey has been consistently professional, knowledgeable, and dedicated to achieving the best possible outcome for me.

One thing he made sure to mention to me, is that if you hire him, you get him. He doesn’t have a bunch of different paralegals doing his work for him while he just shows up on the day of court as other lawyers here in town do. NO! Throughout the whole process, he was always able to answer any questions I had and he guided me through everything in such a detailed manner and had complete open transparency about my case with me at any stage it was in. He was so dedicated to my case, he even showed up to court for me via zoom while he was on vacation on the other side of the world and made sure he was there at the appointed court time even though it was like 3 AM his time.

Thanks to Greg Spivey, I was able to achieve an extremely favorable outcome in my case. I highly recommend Greg Spivey to anyone seeking legal representation. Check out his website, the prices that are listed there are exactly what he charged me. there were no hidden fees or anything like that.  He is an exceptional lawyer and I'm ashamed to say I waited so long to make this review. He’s definitely one of the main reasons that have inspired me to pursue law, graduate from Delmar with my associate degree in criminal justice this year with the intention to transfer next year to a university that offers a law degree so I can help people just like how he helped me.

So thank you, thank you, you definitely helped me make my son's life better by ensuring he gets what is fair and owed.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Maria, thank you so much for all of the wonderful things that you said. It was our pleasure to assist you with your legal situations. You have been a wonderful client to work with. Feel free to come see us any time and let us know how you are doing. I am also glad that we have helped inspire you go to want to go to law school. The world can always use another good honest attorney. Best wishes for your and your family's future.

Gregory Spivey

reviews.text:
I went to this Law Firm, and met Jacob, this was well over a year, maybe even two years ago in the year 2023 I believe. Mr. Guzman was his assistant and they were very helpful. I don’t know what happened but the lawyer, I had , that I was trying to. get away from lied, told me that Jacob and the Huseman Law Firm, said they were not going to even be involved in my case. Jacob had told me I believe that he would take the case and I thought he was going help transition me to his Law Firm, but something  happened, , and the previous lawyer, that I was trying to remove, I believe told some lies and made it to where I would stick with him.. He was going to get all the papers that I gave to Jacob and the Law Firm and have a runner pick them up or something, but I got very angry, And I told them, Mr. Guzman, specifically that I brought those papers there and that they were mine and he was not to give them to the previous lawyer or anyone else.  I was rude and very angry about it.  So they sent them back to me via certified mail. So I do believe the previous lawyer was the one at fault, and he caused a lot of confusion and a lot of angriness on my part. Unfortunately., Mr. Hubert, and Mr. Guzman and the Huesman Law Firm got cheated out of a case, if they were indeed going to take it, because the previous Lawyer told me that they would not take it and were  not going to take it at all and were never going to be involved so I believe it was  Him who caused such confusion, and mixed up othings up, to the point where I thought Mr. Hubert had lied to me. and caused me to be angry with Jacob and Mr. Guzman and the Huseman
Law Firm. He also  cheated me out of a chance, to have a better lawyer, and to see how I would really like the Huseman Law Firm. Unfortunately, I didn’t get that Chance. I wrote a negative review a while back about all this and I shouldn’t have, and if you did Read it, I am retracting anything I ever said there… I feel that I was just cheated out of a chance to use them because the prior lawyer twisted things around to his point of view. Now, if Mr. Hubert and the Law Firm, were not going to take my case, which Jacob led me to believe they would,  I would’ve thought they would’ve told me upfront and face-to-face. But I never got that from them, and all they got from me was an angry., So-called new client, because of something that the prior lawyer mixed up and lied about. I am very sorry that I did not give them a fair shake at Huseman.  I just thought they had abandoned me in midstream.   Now I think I know different..  They seemed very professional there, and I wish I could try and use them again. but I’m sure that bridge is burned.
